<<geometry of sacred design>>

The golden rectangle is formed with a width of 2 meters. The length is calculated using the golden ratio φ. The area of the rectangle is found by multiplying the width by the length. The diagonal of the rectangle is determined using the Pythagorean theorem. The golden ratio φ is (1 + √5) / 2. The length is 2 * φ. The area is 2 * 2φ. The diagonal is √(2² + (2φ)²). The exact length of the diagonal is √(4 + 4φ²). The area of the rectangle is 4φ.

* * *

The circle inscribed within the rectangle has a radius equal to half the width. The radius is 1 meter. The area of the circle is πr². The area of the circle is π * 1². The area inside the rectangle but outside the circle is the area of the rectangle minus the area of the circle. The area of the rectangle is 4φ. The area of the circle is π. The area of the region is 4φ - π. The radius of the inscribed circle is exactly 1 meter. The area of the circle is π square meters. The area of the region is 4φ - π.

* * *
